item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved the containment initialism - CNSI, Compact Non-Sentient Inert - this seems like believable procedure.

item.title: Re: My favorite part
item.content:encoded: I'm waiting for someone at an MC&D event to order a double rum-and-ghost.

item.title: My favorite part
item.content:encoded:
Is imagining what happened to the first guy. I had to look up how many ml are in a 12 Oz can, and there's just enough info in this article to make me feel dread when I imagine the poor shmuck shotgunning 710 ml.

I'm curious what happens when you mix this. I wonder how this is made and marketed because most sodas are also made in a concentrated syrup for soda fountains. What would ghost syrup do to you?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this article, and upvoted, but this sentence

SCP-2107 has to date only been encountered packaged by the individual can.

feels really out of place where it is currently. I think it would be more appropriate to move it to the next paragraph, where the packaging is described. I haven't seen any other comments talking abut this, though, so maybe it's just me :la

Other than that, though, really solid article, +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2107 cans are the traditional variety used for soft drinks, being uniform in design and non-anomalous in composition and behavior.

So 355 ml.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is way too good, congratulations. It has clear and flat description of all the elements within it. However, I missed just one thing: how many milliliters is this can capable of holding?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guess this is how the Foundation get their founds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ahahahahaa yes

that was a part I really liked too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For larger quantities or in cases where a retailer's proprietor is deemed likely to become problematic, Acquisition Protocols 0014 ("Covert Theft"), 0015 ("Overt Theft"), and 0047 ("Impersonation of Federal Agency") may be utilized with authorization from staff with security clearance 3/2107 or higher.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly really enjoy the idea of this one. When I first read the title, I was thinking that this would better fit as a -J, but was I wrong. There is so much mystery with this simple idea of a very spooky soda, especially the last entry in the test log. Very well done. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone else notice that the marketing of this stuff seems exclusively aimed at the Foundation? There's no mention of it having affected anyone that wasn't an agent or at site 19.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I saw the

"100% MORE SPOOKY!"

I thought "THE PUNNENING IS HERE"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read this after it made its way to being a featured skip.

I can't point to one specific thing that made me like this skip, but rather the whole piece kind of comes together to form a very entertaining article.

+1

2sp00ky4me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one quite a bit. It's goofy enough to stick out, but well written to the point that it fits in. Well done.

+1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you don't like it then that's fair, can't argue that. But I'd say this is appropriate as a mainlister and not a -J by current site standards. The tone is how anything on mainlist would be handled, as a dangerous item is a dangerous item regardless of its humorous aspects. I'd say as long as the mainlist has things like Ice Cream Hell, which I totally support as being a mainlist article, I'm working in fairly acceptable space with this one ;)

It's also not a mainlist requirement to be outright scary, and I wasn't overly concerned with making it so. There's more to it than "SODA WOT STEALS YER ORGANS" (and note that this effect is only recorded to have occurred once, meaning it's not an end-all be-all result from drinking this stuff) but if it didn't get across to you then I could see the article coming off as shallow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I seem to be the only one of the opinion that this should be a -j instead of mainlist. It's humorous, but I don't get anything out of it, except that it's a joke. It doesn't really scare me.

It's trying to be funny, but maintain the mainlist critical tone at the same time, and it doesn't seem to really work.

SODA WOT STEALS YER ORGANS just doesn't do anything for me.

Downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: when i saw the tm i thought it was a wondertainmentt product but found my self pleasantly surprised a good scp in general although i prefer ghosts zero, zero calories real ghost taste

item.title: Re: This Can't End Well
item.content:encoded: The can and name are non-anomalous in nature. The liquid contains the anomalous properties. The D-Class would get haunted, but the Foundation may receive a Cease & Desist for copyright infringement, fraud, and metaphysical patent theft.

item.title: This Can't End Well
item.content:encoded: What if the foundation moved the soda from the original can to some sort of knock-off can (eg. Diet Spooks(tm)) and tried to market it to a D-Class

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But those are regular goats. Diet Goats® is made with guaranteed all-natural, gluten free diet goats. Whole different species, same great flavor!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course "Diet Goats" would be a failure. Goats, by their nature, eat everything, and thus inherently cannot diet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's nice to see this one gaining a bit more traction than its sister soda, "DIET GOATS."

item.title: Re: Image Credits
item.content:encoded:
2spoopy.

I take it this is the picture my computer didn't let me see in chat?

item.title: Image Credits
item.content:encoded:
Proposed Article Image
– hide block

Credits:

http://giallo86.deviantart.com/art/free-Soda-Can-Psd-mockup-315570285

David Shetterly’s Double Feature font.

Composition by me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was pretty good until the organ stealing, then it became great. I especially like the possibility that the new instances are just a marketing ploy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I see what you mean about the effect descriptions. Something more along the lines of:

Subject reported audiovisual phenomena (including "hearing [her] name spoken softly," "shadowy figures" at the edges of her vision, and a "flickering of the lights" within the containment chamber that was not corroborated by the recorded footage.)

It's minor tweaks but puts the descriptions into quotes where they feel more natural. It also adds distinction between audiovisual and physiological phenomena.

E: Adjustments made to all trial logs that I believe tighten up the tone and flow overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the effects could be described with more clinical terms, but this could really be the next Moth Helm. It's perfectly wacky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a well-written series 1 feel to it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this quite a bit, and it has a bunch of subtle hooks built in. Like where is Original Ghost™? Just how many Acquisition Protocols are there? And how did poor H██ T████ guzzle down two whole cans before ???? happened to him?

+All the upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It mixes funny and terrifying at the same time a very well written page

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool. Very cool. I want a can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just bizarre enough to tip it over the edge of ambivalence into tentative upvote.

item.title: Creepycola
item.content:encoded:
So yeah. Thanks to CharonTheHand, TwistedGears, Sophia Light, LlamaNumbers, theworstninja, JackIII, and Logan Armstrong for giving this looks in chat or the forum. This one is definitely odd and not something I really ever expected to write when I joined but I'm glad it happened. Enjoy™.

E: Massive thanks to Faminepulse for the picture! Individual credits for source materials for the image can be seen in his post below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not often an SCP is both funny and scary at the same time. +1.
